old_claim.note: Newspaper Periodicals provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The scoped practice is paid notices inserted into a recurring newspaper; the Boston News-Letter solicited advertisements in its first weekly issue and carried the first paid ads two issues later.

source_shape.source_locator: MHS says the Boston News-Letter began as the first regularly published newspaper in British North America, solicited small notices at a reasonable rate in its first issue, and carried the first three paid advertisements in issue number three dated 1-8 May 1704.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source ties paid public newspaper advertisements to a recurring newspaper that solicited notices and then printed paid notices.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the target node is specifically newspaper advertising, the cited periodical platform is the direct publishing mechanism rather than just a parallel communication medium.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: public_newspaper_advertising remains enabled by newspaper_periodicals, now with source-backed evidence and a narrower paid-newspaper-notice scope.
would_reject_if: The node is later broadened to all printed advertising rather than notices inserted into recurring newspapers.
A stronger source shows the cited 1704 notices were not paid newspaper advertisements.
A more specific Boston News-Letter advertising node is added and this broad node is moved to later classified-ad systems.
short_reason: The recurring newspaper is the source-backed delivery mechanism for the paid notices.
